pulse: The energy sector currently trades at a median EV/EBITDA of 10.6x and a P/E of 18.1x, showing a slight premium compared to the broader market. Recent headlines surrounding the Iran conflict have led to oil prices rising to six-month highs, benefiting companies like ExxonMobil (XOM) and Chevron (CVX), which have EV/EBITDA multiples of 11.4x and 11.2x, respectively, but also demonstrate strained profit margins amid rising operational risks. As geopolitical tensions persist, investors should closely monitor supply chain disruptions and their potential impact on revenue growth and margin resilience within the sector, particularly for firms reliant on Middle Eastern oil imports.
